summary: Frontend-only testing of two P1 features: (1) Quick-view modal on product cards and (2) Wishlist sync to backend for logged-in users. Both features pass end-to-end. Quick View dialog opens/closes correctly on /shop and / (home NEW IN), qty controls work (1→3→2), wishlist toggle from inside dialog persists to localStorage, Add to Cart inside dialog adds line item and closes the dialog while opening the cart drawer, View Full Details routes to /product/:slug, close button + Escape keyboard both close the dialog. Wishlist server sync verified end-to-end: guest toggles persist to localStorage; after login GET /api/wishlist contains the merged IDs (guest-local merged up); add toggle while logged in POSTs to server and shows in GET; remove toggle DELETEs and disappears from GET; /account/wishlist renders the items with images/prices/Move to Bag/Remove actions; after logout localStorage retains IDs (intended).

minor.issue: Observed a transient 502 once when posting linen-cushion-cover during add-toggle. The next GET /api/wishlist still showed the item present, so the request was retried/succeeded eventually. Worth noting but not reproducible deterministically.

issues: Radix Dialog console warnings: `DialogContent` requires a `DialogTitle` for accessibility (screen reader users). Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby` on DialogContent. Add a VisuallyHidden DialogTitle + DialogDescription (or wire aria-describedby) to silence Radix warnings and improve a11y.

action_items: Add `DialogTitle` (can be VisuallyHidden) inside `QuickViewDialog` to satisfy Radix a11y requirement and remove the runtime console warnings. Optionally add a `DialogDescription` or `aria-describedby` reference.

Optional: investigate the transient 502 on POST /api/wishlist (only seen once). Existing client-side catch-and-ignore is fine since the toggle is local-first; consider a single retry to make wishlist sync more robust to flaky network.
critical_code_review_comments: WishlistContext correctly imports useAuth from AuthContext and uses a `mergedRef` to ensure the local→server merge runs once per auth transition. Reset of `mergedRef.current = false` on logout/no-user is correct so a subsequent login re-merges.
toggle() applies server sync inside setIds updater using a stale closure capture of `user`. Because useCallback depends on `[user]` the function is recreated on auth changes, so this is correct.

ProductCard hoists QuickViewDialog inside the same article fragment — good. Quick View button is hover-revealed via opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 which works on desktop; on touch devices the button is still in the DOM and tappable. Verified by Playwright `force=True` click.
QuickViewDialog uses shadcn Dialog which closes on backdrop click and Escape — both verified in test.

QuickViewDialog renders heading text in a regular <h2> but Radix expects a `<DialogTitle>` for accessibility — add it (can wrap with VisuallyHidden if visual layout must stay unchanged, or reuse the existing <h2> by swapping to DialogTitle).

addItem from CartContext is called with (product, qty, { colour }) in QuickViewDialog — verify CartContext.addItem accepts an options object (it appears to, since the cart drawer correctly shows the line at qty 2 after the test). Good.
Wishlist localStorage is intentionally retained after logout per spec — verified.
test_credentials: Customer: customer@mahomeinteriors.com / customer12345 (verified, logged in, server wishlist persisted across runs)
seed_data_creation: None. Used seeded products (luna-table-lamp, nola-glass-pendant, natura-coffee-table, linen-cushion-cover).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Quick View testid pattern: quickview-open-{product.id}; dialog testid: quickview-dialog; controls: quickview-qty / quickview-qty-inc / quickview-qty-dec / quickview-add / quickview-wishlist / quickview-close / quickview-view-details. Button is hover-revealed (opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100). Use force=True or .hover() before click. Wishlist endpoints (already verified previously): GET /api/wishlist, POST /api/wishlist {product_id}, DELETE /api/wishlist/{product_id}. Customer test account is seeded with persistent server-side wishlist between runs (luna, nola, natura observed at start of run 2). /account/wishlist URL renders products and exposes 'Move to Bag' and 'Remove' actions per item. Iteration 1 backend issue (GET /api/checkout/status returning 500) was NOT re-tested in this iteration — verify separately if a checkout retest is needed.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no blocking bugs. Only an a11y warning from Radix Dialog due to missing DialogTitle inside QuickViewDialog. Fix: import { DialogTitle, DialogDescription } from './ui/dialog' and add <DialogTitle className='sr-only'>{product.name}</DialogTitle> (or wrap the existing <h2> as DialogTitle) inside DialogContent.
